title: Gentle Simmered Moroccan Bread Rolls Briouat
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring soft and pillowy bread rolls simmered in a sweet and spicy sauce, perfect for a comforting snack or dessert. This low-fat-friendly version is a healthier take on the classic recipe.
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger, cinnamon, sugar, water, lemon juice, ras el hanout, and salt, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook for 10 minutes.
Add the bread rolls to the saucepan and simmer, covered, for 15 minutes, or until the rolls are soft and pillowy.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the briouat warm, garnished with a sprinkle of cinnamon and a drizzle of honey, if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
